notes: Scenario 2.19 is the EXPLICIT permission-gate prompt arc: a tool call requiring permission opens the picker (workingÔćĺwaiting), the user resolves it (waitingÔćĺworking), the tool runs, and the turn completes (workingÔćĺready). kiro-cli 2.5.1 performs this in full ÔÇö it raises a 'write requires approval' picker (Yes / Trust always in session / No; FINDINGS.md ┬ž6) for every mutating call by default (read-only auto-runs). The recipe launches plain `kiro-cli chat` (NO --trust-all-tools), asks kiro to write a file (so the picker appears), drives the picker via `keys` to select 'Yes', and the turn completes. The spec asserts the CORRECT arc readyÔćĺworkingÔćĺwaitingÔćĺworkingÔćĺready. KNOWN FAILING on the load-bearing waiting observable: while the picker is pending the kiro JSONL already holds the AssistantMessage with the open `write` toolUse and NO further events until approval (FINDINGS.md ┬ž2/┬ž5), but the daemon classifies an open tool with no result as working (state_classifier.go rule 4). The only fallback to waiting for a held edit-permission is OpenToolStalled (rule 1b), gated by isPermissionGatedEditTool which matches the exact names Edit/Write/MultiEdit/NotebookEdit (session.go:268) ÔÇö kiro's lowercase write/shell do NOT match, so the pending-approval window never reaches waiting. The local trace plainly carries the open write call, so this is a parser/allowlist bug (extend the permission-gated edit-tool set to kiro's lowercase names), not architectural incapability; kiro has no permission hook (PermissionPending is claudecode-only, rule 0 cannot fire). record files the issue and records known_failing; the spec asserts the correct behavior unweakened. The kiro JSONL carries no model/token/cost (sidecar-only, not read by the v1 parser; FINDINGS.md ┬ž4), so observations are model=unknown, cost_nonzero=false, tokens_nonzero=false.

text: kiro-cli chat session appears in ready
User prompt asking kiro to write a file drives ready Ôćĺ working

KNOWN FAILING: the write-approval picker is pending ÔÇö the open `write` toolUse is in the transcript with no result until approval, so the session SHOULD be waiting. The daemon currently stays working because kiro's lowercase `write` is not in the permission-gated edit-tool allowlist (isPermissionGatedEditTool, session.go:268) and kiro emits no permission hook ÔÇö so OpenToolStalled never sets and rule 0 cannot fire. Spec asserts the CORRECT waiting state.
User answers the picker ('Yes') Ôćĺ the write tool executes and emits its toolResult Ôćĺ waiting Ôćĺ working
Turn completes back to ready (text-only AssistantMessage); final state is ready
